calendar.title: Calendar

calendar.noWorkspace: Select a workspace to view calendar

notificationSettings.description: Choose how you want to be notified for each event type.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: ito is a thread-based task management tool. Create a workspace, invite your team, and start connecting tasks.
gettingStarted.step1: Click the workspace switcher in the top-left of the sidebar.
gettingStarted.step2: Click "New workspace" and enter a name.
gettingStarted.step3: Your workspace is ready. Start creating tasks.
gettingStarted.inviteStep1: Go to Workspace Settings from the sidebar.
gettingStarted.inviteStep2: Enter the email address of the person you want to invite.
gettingStarted.inviteStep3: They will receive an invite link. Once they accept, they join your workspace.

taskManagement.title: Task Management
taskManagement.description: Tasks are the basic unit of work in ito. Create tasks, set priorities, assign due dates, and track progress.
taskManagement.createStep1: Click the "New Task" button or use the quick input at the bottom.
taskManagement.createStep2: Enter a title, set priority and due date.
taskManagement.createStep3: The task is automatically assigned to you. You can then connect it to someone else.

threadConnection.title: Thread Connection
threadConnection.description: Threads are the core of ito. Connect a task to another person to delegate work. The chain can extend through multiple people.
threadConnection.step1: You create a task — you are the assignee.
threadConnection.step2: Click "Connect" to pass it to a team member. They become the new assignee.
threadConnection.step3: That person can connect it further, building a chain (A → B → C).
threadConnection.step4: When the last person resolves their part, it snaps back through the chain.
threadConnection.rule1: You cannot connect a thread to yourself.
threadConnection.rule2: Only the current assignee can connect to the next person.
threadConnection.rule3: You cannot create a loop with active participants in the chain.

snapback.title: Snap-back System
snapback.description: When someone resolves their thread link, the task automatically returns to the previous person in the chain. This continues until it reaches the original creator.
snapback.example1: A creates a task and connects it to B.
snapback.example2: B connects it to C. Now the chain is A → B → C.
snapback.example3: C finishes and resolves. The task snaps back to B.
snapback.example4: B reviews and resolves. The task snaps back to A.
snapback.example5: A marks the task as COMPLETED. The workflow is done.
snapback.tip: You will receive a notification every time a thread snaps back to you. Check the Notifications page to stay on top of your tasks.

blockers.title: Blockers
blockers.description: Register external dependencies that block your task. The task moves to 'Waiting' until you resolve the blocker.
blockers.step1: Open a task and click 'Connect', then switch to 'Blocker' tab
blockers.step2: Describe the external dependency (e.g., 'Waiting for design review')
blockers.step3: When the blocker is resolved, click the 'Resolve Blocker' button on the task
blockers.note: Only the person who created the blocker can resolve it.

taskGroups.title: Task Groups
taskGroups.description: Organize tasks into groups, similar to Slack channels. Groups appear in the sidebar under 'My Tasks'.
taskGroups.step1: Click the '+' button under 'My Tasks' in the sidebar to create a new group
taskGroups.step2: Click a group name to view only tasks in that group
taskGroups.step3: Click 'My Tasks' to see all tasks regardless of group

slack.title: Slack Integration
slack.description: Use the /ito command in Slack to create and manage tasks directly from your chat.

chain.description: Create a task and connect a thread chain

link.description: Link your Slack account to ito

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: ito sends real-time notifications so you never miss important updates. Notifications appear in-app and can also be sent via email or Slack.

threadSnapped.description: A thread you sent out has been resolved and returned to you.

threadCompleted.name: Thread Completed
threadCompleted.description: A thread chain you were part of has been fully completed.

welcome.title: Welcome to ito!
welcome.description: Ready to start thread-based collaboration? Create tasks and connect threads to your team — completed work automatically snaps back to you.

seedData.title: Create sample data?
seedData.description: We'll generate example tasks so you can experience ito's core features right away with realistic project tasks.

taskOverview.title: Check your tasks
taskOverview.description: The generated tasks are now visible in the list. Each task is managed by status (in progress, completed, etc.) and priority.

threadConcept.title: Try connecting threads
threadConcept.description: Thread connection is the core of ito. Pass tasks to team members to create chains — completed work snaps back automatically.

completion.title: All set!
completion.description: Now create your own tasks, invite team members, and start connecting threads.
completion.step2: Invite team members to your workspace

meta.title: ito - Thread-based Task Collaboration
meta.description: Connect teammates with threads, hand off tasks, and automatically get them back. A new way to manage teamwork.
meta.keywords: task management, team collaboration, thread-based, project management, task delegation, workflow automation
